cross_references.category_mapping: breaking → BREAKING_KINDS | api_break → API_BREAK_KINDS | risk → RISK_KINDS | addition → ADDITION_KINDS | quality → QUALITY_KINDS | no_change → (empty)
cross_references.crosscheck_engine: abicheck/buildsource/crosscheck.py — run_crosschecks() produces the eight ADR-035 D4/D8 cross-source / audit ChangeKinds; the G20 corpus demonstrates seven of them, and public_to_internal_dependency is demonstrated intra-version by case181_xcheck_public_to_internal_dependency
cross_references.evidence_tiers: scripts/evidence_tiers.py — EVIDENCE_TIER_BY_KIND + KINDLESS_CASE_TIER compute min_evidence; benchmark_comparison.py --evidence-tiers runs the catalog per tier
cross_references.examples_readme: examples/README.md — case index table (must stay in sync)
cross_references.g20_fixture_writer: scripts/gen_g20_fixtures.py — single source of truth for the committed snapshot fixtures (snapshot.abi.json) of cases 143-151 and case181
cross_references.test_autodiscovery: tests/test_example_autodiscovery.py — reads EXPECTED from this file
cross_references.test_crosscheck_catalog: tests/test_g20_catalog.py — validates each G20 fixture against canonical expected_kinds and provider_assertions via run_crosschecks
cross_references.test_evidence_tiers: tests/test_evidence_tiers.py — keeps min_evidence in sync with scripts/evidence_tiers.py
cross_references.test_manual: tests/test_abi_examples.py — hardcoded expectations for cases 01-18 (must stay in sync)
cross_references.verdict_classification: abicheck/checker_policy.py — BREAKING_KINDS, API_BREAK_KINDS, RISK_KINDS, COMPATIBLE_KINDS (= ADDITION_KINDS ∪ QUALITY_KINDS)

description: Ground-truth verdict and change kinds for all abicheck examples. Every case has exactly one canonical expected verdict and one canonical expected_kinds set, invariant across compiler, workflow, artifact variant, and evidence depth. A lane that cannot observe that truth reports a scoped detection gap; it never supplies another expectation. expected_absent_kinds are negative false-positive guards. min_evidence is the first evidence layer able to prove the canonical truth (L0 binary / L1 +debug / L2 +headers / L3 +build / L4 +source / L5 +source-graph). Audit cases use the same expected/expected_kinds contract; provider_assertions describes provider agreement, not another truth. Bundle library assertions are structural checks beneath the single case-level verdict.

case100_experimental_removed_without_rep.description: A declaration only ever lived under lib::experimental:: in v1 and is removed from v2 with no stable-namespace replacement. The dedicated EXPERIMENTAL_REMOVED_WITHOUT_REPLACEMENT finding tells reviewers there is no migration target; the underlying mangled symbol disappearance is also reported as a plain func_removed.

case101_inline_namespace_version_bumped.description: Library uses a versioned inline namespace `_V1` in v1 and bumps to `_V2` in v2. Declarations look identical to consumers so source keeps compiling, but every freshly built TU emits mangled symbols carrying the new version segment — old and new TUs of the same program ODR-violate. Specialisation of the existing inline_namespace_moved that fires from declared-name evidence so it works on header-only inputs and single-symbol bumps.

case102_frozen_runtime_signature_changed.description: An extern "C" runtime entry inside a contractually-frozen namespace (mirrors oneTBB's `detail::r1`) has its parameter and return types widened in place. The exported symbol name is unchanged but callers built against v1 pass int into what is now a long slot. Detected by the existing FUNC_PARAMS_CHANGED / FUNC_RETURN_CHANGED detectors; the new `frozen_namespaces:` policy field additionally tags the finding via Change.frozen_namespace_violation and refuses any `overrides:` entry that would downgrade it. See policy.yaml in the case directory.

case103_toolchain_flag_drift.description: Public surface and every exported symbol are identical between v1 and v2; only the ABI-affecting compiler flag set differs (v2 adds -fshort-enums). The toolchain-flag drift detector parses DW_AT_producer and reports the mismatch as a compatible, informational quality signal — a leading indicator that the two builds were made under different ABI assumptions, before that drift turns into a real layout break on a library that exposes enums/packed structs/a different data model.
case103_toolchain_flag_drift.known_gap: Clang: the toolchain-flag drift signal reads the compiler flag set from DW_AT_producer, but clang does not embed command-line flags there by default (it needs -grecord-command-line), so for this default-clang fixture -fshort-enums is invisible and the tool returns NO_CHANGE. GCC records the switches in DW_AT_producer by default, so the drift is detected. When clang IS built with -grecord-command-line the flags are embedded and abicheck detects the drift (and the artifact-level enum_underlying_size_changed) — proven end-to-end by tests/test_compiler_record_cross_toolchain.py::test_clang_grecord_command_line_flag_drift_detected (G23-C). Detection therefore requires GCC or a clang build with -grecord-command-line; abicheck also unions the flag set across all CUs so a flag applied to only some translation units is not missed.

case104_glibcxx_dual_abi_flip.description: Identical C++ source built once with _GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI=0 (v1) and once with =1 (v2). GNU libstdc++ ships two incompatible std::string/std::list ABIs selected by this macro, so every std::string-bearing exported symbol gets a different mangled name (std::__cxx11::basic_string vs the legacy std::basic_string). abicheck recognises the mass-churn signature and collapses it into a single glibcxx_dual_abi_flip_detected diagnostic alongside the underlying func_removed/func_added evidence; verdict is BREAKING. GNU libstdc++ only.

case105_concept_tightening.description: A C++20 `concept` (`Addable`) gains an additional requirement (default-constructibility). Already-shipped binary instantiations keep linking, but consumers instantiating `sum<T>` for types that fail the tightened constraint no longer compile. The canonical truth is API_BREAK. It is missed by default object/DWARF and header/castxml comparison (castxml emits concepts as `<Unimplemented kind="Concept"/>`, no name, no body), but visible at L4: source-ABI replay reports `concept_tightened` (API_BREAK) when that opt-in evidence is supplied.
case105_concept_tightening.known_gap: L0-L2 artifact/header scans cannot observe the C++20 concept constraint because CastXML emits concepts as unnamed <Unimplemented> nodes. Their NO_CHANGE verdict is a missed API_BREAK detection caused by insufficient evidence, not a lane-specific expectation. L4 source-ABI replay emits the named concept and constraint hash and detects concept_tightened.

source_smoke.proof: consumer concept-tightening smoke: old accepted wrapped, new rejects tightened requires-expression

case106_ctor_became_explicit.description: A converting conversion operator on a public handle type (mirrors `operator int()` on a oneTBB handle) gains the `explicit` specifier. The mangled name is unchanged so previously-compiled binaries still link, but any source code that wrote `int n = ta;` or relied on implicit conversion at a call site fails to compile against v2. Detected by the new CTOR_EXPLICIT_ADDED ChangeKind, derived from DW_AT_explicit on DW_TAG_subprogram (and `explicit="1"` on castxml Method/Constructor — but not Converter, see known_gap).
case106_ctor_became_explicit.known_gap: castxml does not emit the `explicit` attribute on <Converter> elements (user-defined conversion operators), and does not emit a mangled name for user-declared constructors. The CTOR_EXPLICIT_ADDED detector is therefore not exercised end-to-end by the default castxml-based dump path on Linux/macOS — only via DWARF-only mode. The detector itself is validated by tests/test_explicit_ctor.py with synthetic snapshots.

source_smoke.proof: consumer explicit-conversion smoke: old implicit conversion compiles, new explicit conversion rejects it

case107_task_scheduler_init_removed.description: Named regression fixture for oneTBB's largest historical ABI break: tbb::task_scheduler_init was removed in oneTBB 2021.1. The entire publicly-exported class and all of its members disappear. Detected by the existing FUNC_REMOVED / TYPE_REMOVED detectors.

case108_task_class_removed.description: Named regression fixture for the oneTBB 2021.1 removal of the polymorphic `tbb::task` base class. Removing a publicly-derivable polymorphic base destroys every user subclass's vtable, breaks RTTI across DSOs, and undefines the typeinfo symbol. Detected by existing FUNC_REMOVED / TYPE_REMOVED detectors.

case109_flow_graph_policy_renames.description: Header-only policy-tag types used as template parameters for `function_node` are renamed wholesale (queueing/rejecting → buffering_policy/backpressure_policy) and the instantiation-anchor typedef is dropped. The example deliberately keeps v1.cpp / v2.cpp byte-identical (no template instantiations) so the .so symbol table is unchanged across the version pair; any rebuild against v2 headers still fails. Detected via `typedef_removed` + `type_removed` (both BREAKING per change_registry). Member of the oneTBB regression suite; mirrors the 2021 flow::graph policy reshuffle.
case109_flow_graph_policy_renames.known_gap: macOS/Homebrew castxml currently drops the header-only typedef/type-removal signal for this fixture, so the macOS nightly observes NO_CHANGE even with L2 headers. Linux remains the blocking detector lane.

case110_concurrent_unordered_map_api_dri.description: Public member function `insert` loses one of its parameters between versions. The old mangled symbol disappears, the new overload has a different mangled name — both source and link break. Member of the oneTBB regression suite; mirrors historical signature tightenings on `tbb::concurrent_unordered_map`.

case111_enumerable_thread_specific_lambd.description: A second constructor overload accepting `std::function<int()>` is added next to the existing `int`-form constructor. By itself this is a pure addition — existing call sites that pass an int still resolve to the original constructor. The risk: brace-initialization and generic callable arguments can become ambiguous at downstream call sites. Member of the oneTBB regression suite.

source_smoke.proof: consumer overload-resolution smoke: old brace-init compiles, new lambda/factory overload is ambiguous

case112_lp64_ilp64.description: A BLAS/LAPACK-style integer interface (as shipped by e.g. oneMKL) switched LP64 (INT=int, 32-bit) to ILP64 (INT=long, 64-bit). A large fraction of public integer parameters/returns flip width together and the integer typedef resizes, the signature of an LP64/ILP64 model switch.

case113_abi_tag_changed.description: A single C++ symbol loses its Itanium [abi:cxx11] ABI tag ([[gnu::abi_tag]]). The demangled name is unchanged but the mangled symbol (B5cxx11 component) differs, so consumers linked against the tagged symbol fail to resolve it.

case114_char8t_migration.description: A public UTF-8 API migrated from char-family spellings to C++20 char8_t on parameters and a struct field. char8_t is a distinct type (mangling Du), so the mangled symbols change and old binaries fail to resolve them.

case115_bit_int_width_changed.description: A public C23 _BitInt(64) accumulator widened to _BitInt(128) across a struct field, a by-value parameter, and a return type. The bit width is part of the type, so storage size and calling-convention treatment change. (128 is clang's maximum _BitInt width, keeping the case portable.)

case116_atomic_qualifier_changed.description: A public struct field and a return type gained the C11 _Atomic qualifier. Per WG14 the size/alignment of an _Atomic-qualified type may differ from the unqualified type and varies across compilers, so layout and the return ABI diverge.

case117_no_unique_address.description: An empty member gained [[no_unique_address]] in C++20, letting the compiler overlay it with the next member. No dedicated ChangeKind is needed: the overlay shows up as the existing type_size_changed / type_field_offset_changed layout kinds.

case121_kernel_btf_struct_field_added.description: Linux kernel BTF: a vmlinux struct (task_state) grows from 2 to 3 fields between kernel releases, changing sizeof 8→12. An out-of-tree module / eBPF CO-RE program built against the old layout accesses wrong offsets. Committed BTF blobs (v1.btf/v2.btf) are parsed by parse_btf_from_bytes and diffed through the same layout detectors as DWARF.
case121_kernel_btf_struct_field_added.reason: committed BTF blobs (not compilable C/C++ source); validated end-to-end via tests/test_workflow_kernel_accel.py::test_committed_btf_example_matches_ground_truth and the gcc -gbtf integration fixture

case122_template_signature_uninstantiate.description: An uninstantiated function template's parameter types change (int -> long). A real source/API break for consumers of the template. The canonical truth is COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK. It is missed by default object/DWARF and header/castxml comparison (nothing is emitted to compare), but visible at L4: source-ABI replay parses the real clang AST, hashes each template's parameter list, and reports the mismatch as template_body_changed (RISK) — demonstrating that this class of C++-only change needs source evidence, not that it is undetectable outright. Seeing it takes explicit L3 compile-unit + public-header evidence (a bare --sources tree with no build metadata is not enough) plus --no-scope-public-headers on compare, since the L2 header backend does not model templates and would otherwise scope the finding out as internal.
case122_template_signature_uninstantiate.known_gap: L0-L2 artifact/header scans cannot observe the uninstantiated function template because it emits no symbol and CastXML omits the declaration. Their NO_CHANGE verdict is a missed COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK detection caused by insufficient evidence, not a lane-specific expectation. L4 source-ABI replay parses the real Clang AST and detects template_body_changed.

case123_default_argument_removed.description: A function's default argument is removed in v2. The mangled symbol is identical (defaults don't affect mangling), so binaries keep running, but callers relying on the default no longer compile. Detected only in header mode via the param_default_value_removed ChangeKind (castxml exposes the Argument `default` attribute); invisible in object/DWARF mode.
case123_default_argument_removed.note: castxml on macOS/Homebrew does not emit the `default=` attribute on <Argument> elements, so the param_default_value_* detection only fires with the Linux castxml build. Scoped to linux to avoid a platform-dependent castxml gap (constant/finality extraction is unaffected and stays cross-platform).

case124_header_constant_value_changed.description: A public constexpr constant changes value (8 -> 16). The value is baked into consumers at compile time, so mixing header versions disagrees on sizes; yet the constant has internal linkage (no symbol), so the binaries are identical and object/DWARF comparison sees nothing. Detected only in header mode via constant_changed: the dumper extracts const/constexpr initializers declared in the provided public headers (castxml `init` attribute). Plain #define macros remain invisible (castxml emits no macros).
case124_header_constant_value_changed.known_gap: macOS/Homebrew castxml does not emit the constexpr initializer signal this case relies on, so the macOS nightly observes NO_CHANGE. Linux remains the blocking detector lane for constant_changed.

case125_class_became_final.description: A public, extensible base class gains the `final` specifier in v2. Layout, vtable and mangled names are unchanged, so already-compiled binaries keep running, but any consumer that derives from it no longer compiles. Detected via the new TYPE_BECAME_FINAL ChangeKind, which reads the `final` class-key from castxml header analysis. Invisible in object/DWARF mode (the binaries are ABI-identical), demonstrating why source/header analysis is required for the full API picture.
case125_class_became_final.known_gap: macOS/Homebrew castxml does not expose the class final marker consistently for this fixture, so the macOS nightly observes NO_CHANGE. Linux remains the blocking detector lane for type_became_final.

source_smoke.proof: consumer final-class smoke: old derived class compiles, new final base rejects derivation

case126_sycl_device_impl_ptr.description: Distilled from intel/llvm PR #20821 (the `abi-break`-labelled change that swapped `std::shared_ptr<device_impl>` for a raw `device_impl*` inside `sycl::device`, surfacing as issue #20915 / fix #21028). The shared_ptr member is two pointers (16 bytes) on LP64; the raw pointer is one (8 bytes), so sizeof(sycl::device) shrinks 16->8 — an ABI break for every consumer that holds a device by value or embeds it. The method mangled names are unchanged, so a pure exported-symbol dump (sycl_symbols_*.dump) does not see the layout change directly; abicheck's type/layout diff reports the root cause as TYPE_SIZE_CHANGED. Built with -fvisibility-inlines-hidden so the diff shows the public ABI break rather than libstdc++ template noise, mirroring how libsycl is built.

case127_data_object_size_changed.description: An exported global data object (config_table) grows from int[16] (64 bytes) to int[32] (128 bytes), changing its ELF symbol st_size. Consumers that reference the object get a copy relocation sized for the v1 definition, so after the upgrade library code that indexes the new slots writes past the consumer's fixed-size copy — silent out-of-bounds corruption. This is the static-variable-size regression the glibc testsuite guards and that Apple's dynamic-library guidelines classify as a major change; detected at L0 from the symbol table alone.

case128_symbol_binding_strengthened.description: The exported function helper changes from weak (STB_WEAK) to strong/global (STB_GLOBAL) binding. This is backward compatible: every binary that resolved to the weak definition still resolves to the same address, and the only difference is that the symbol can no longer be silently overridden by another strong definition. It is the benign counterpart to case27 (binding weakened, which is risky). Detected at L0 from the symbol table.

case129_struct_return_convention.description: A public function returns a small aggregate by value. In v2 the aggregate gains a user-declared destructor, making it non-trivial; by the System V AMD64 ABI a non-trivial class is returned through a hidden caller-provided pointer (sret) instead of in registers. The function's mangled name (_Z7computev) is unchanged, so a symbol-only check sees nothing, but the DWARF value-ABI return trait flips trivial -> nontrivial. abicheck emits struct_return_convention_changed (the return-specific refinement of value_abi_trait_changed); a caller built against the v1 in-register convention reads the result from the wrong place. Detected at L1 from DWARF.

case130_exceptions_mode_flip.description: Identical source and exported symbols built once with -fexceptions (v1) and once with -fno-exceptions (v2). The generated CMake compile_commands.json carries the flag; the L3 build-evidence diff normalizes it to the canonical 'exceptions' option and reports exceptions_mode_changed. The two modes are not link-compatible (exceptions cannot safely unwind through -fno-exceptions frames), but per ADR-028 D3 a build-context finding is a risk signal, not a proven binary break. Validated via the CLI dump --build-info + compare path (the Python compare() API does not run the build-evidence diff).

case131_rtti_mode_flip.description: Identical source and symbols built with -frtti (v1) vs -fno-rtti (v2), captured from the generated CMake compile_commands.json. The L3 diff normalizes to the canonical 'rtti' option and reports rtti_mode_changed. -fno-rtti omits type_info for polymorphic types so dynamic_cast/typeid and cross-DSO exception matching can fail when modes are mixed; RISK per ADR-028 D3.

case132_threadsafe_statics_flip.description: Identical source and symbols built with -fthreadsafe-statics (v1, the default) vs -fno-threadsafe-statics (v2). The L3 diff normalizes to the canonical 'threadsafe_statics' option and reports threadsafe_statics_mode_changed. -fno-threadsafe-statics drops the __cxa_guard acquire/release around function-local static init, so mixed-mode TUs sharing a public inline race on first use; RISK per ADR-028 D3.

case133_tls_model_flip.description: Identical source and symbols built with -ftls-model=global-dynamic (v1) vs -ftls-model=initial-exec (v2). The L3 diff normalizes both to the canonical 'tls_model' option and reports tls_model_changed. The TLS access sequence differs and initial-exec cannot be used from a library dlopen'd after startup; RISK per ADR-028 D3.

case134_relro_weakened.description: Full RELRO (-z relro -z now) dropped to none (-z norelro) — the GNU_RELRO segment disappears, removing read-only-after-relocation hardening (RELRO_WEAKENED). Identical ABI surface.

case135_stack_canary_removed.description: -fstack-protector-all dropped to -fno-stack-protector — __stack_chk_fail guard references disappear, removing stack-smashing protection (STACK_CANARY_REMOVED). Identical ABI surface.

case136_executable_stack_removed.description: Executable stack corrected: v1 GNU_STACK RWE (-z execstack) becomes RW (-z noexecstack) in v2 — the security-fix direction, complementing case49 (EXECUTABLE_STACK_REMOVED). Identical ABI surface.

case137_runpath_changed.description: DT_RUNPATH added in v2 (-rpath with new dtags) — the loader's library search path changes while the exported ABI surface stays identical (RUNPATH_CHANGED).

case138_needed_added.description: DT_NEEDED added in v2 (libm force-linked via --no-as-needed) — a new runtime dependency appears with no change to the exported ABI surface (NEEDED_ADDED).

case139_symbol_version_node_removed.description: Version node LIBX_2.0 (defining beta) deleted in v2; beta folded into LIBX_1.0 — a consumer linked against beta@LIBX_2.0 can no longer resolve its versioned symbol (SYMBOL_VERSION_NODE_REMOVED).

case140_empty_base_optimization_lost.description: An empty `Tag` base gains one member, so the Empty Base Optimization is lost and the unrelated `Payload` base subobject moves from offset 0 to offset 8 (BASE_CLASS_OFFSET_CHANGED). sizeof grows and every later member shifts; a consumer upcasting Widget* to Payload* reads the wrong bytes.

case141_versioned_symbol_scheme.description: Library carries its major version as a suffix on every exported symbol (ICU u_<name>_<major>); v2 bumps the whole API _3 -> _4 at once. Source using a version macro keeps compiling, but the .so renames all symbols, so consumers linked at the same SONAME fail with undefined symbol. abicheck reports the removals (BREAKING) and adds one advisory versioned_symbol_scheme_detected finding; opt-in --collapse-versioned-symbols reclassifies the version-rename pairs to show the real delta.

case142_vtable_slot_count_binary_only.description: A virtual method is inserted in the middle of a polymorphic class, growing the _ZTV vtable object by one pointer. abicheck detects the slot-count change from the ELF symbol size alone — no DWARF, no headers (VTABLE_SLOT_COUNT_CHANGED, evidence tier L0).

case143_audit_accidental_export.description: Single-release audit (no baseline): a function defined with default visibility but never declared in a public header is an accidental export. Crosschecking the binary export table against the public-header AST flags it (EXPORTED_NOT_PUBLIC); neither source alone sees it.

case144_audit_private_header_leak.description: Single-release audit: a public API returns detail::WidgetImpl*, a type defined only in a private (non-installed) header. The public-header AST cross-check flags the un-installed-type leak (PRIVATE_HEADER_LEAK).

case145_audit_unversioned_export.description: Single-release audit, pure L0: the library defines a symbol-versioning scheme (DEMO_1.0) but a new export ships with no version node. It cannot be evolved compatibly later (UNVERSIONED_EXPORTED_SYMBOL) - read from the export table vs .gnu.version_d alone.

case146_audit_rtti_for_internal.description: Single-release audit: _ZTI/_ZTV (typeinfo + vtable) are exported for InternalNode, a type declared only in a private header. Consumers cannot name the type yet its RTTI is on the ABI surface (RTTI_FOR_INTERNAL_TYPE) - export table cross-checked against header provenance.

case147_scan_depth_ladder.description: Depth-ladder legibility case: the same input answered at increasing depth. An S3 lexical pattern scan flags a risky construct; the S5 source graph + replay confirm a genuine PRIVATE_HEADER_LEAK and add the source_index provider. The README's honest-coverage block shows exactly what each depth proved.

case148_xcheck_header_build_mismatch.description: Cross-source corroboration (flagship): the headers were parsed without the build's ABI-relevant flags (glibcxx_use_cxx11_abi, -DBIG_BUFFERS), so the recorded layout cannot be trusted. Only L2 macros cross-checked against L3 build flags expose the divergence (HEADER_BUILD_CONTEXT_MISMATCH); binary-only is blind.

case149_xcheck_odr_variant.description: Cross-source corroboration: two translation units materialize one public type (geometry::Vec3) with different layouts. Only the L4 per-TU source-ABI surface cross-checking layout against layout records the ODR conflict (ODR_TYPE_VARIANT); no single artifact layer sees it.

case150_xcheck_export_public_pair.description: Bidirectional L0-exports vs L2-decls cross-check: one symbol is exported with no public declaration (EXPORTED_NOT_PUBLIC) and one public-header declaration is never exported because a static definition slipped in (PUBLIC_NOT_EXPORTED). The combination of the two sources reveals both halves of the contract mismatch.

case151_xcheck_provider_matrix.description: Provider-agreement matrix: the same PRIVATE_HEADER_LEAK finding corroborated by two providers in the rich fixture (public_header_ast + source_index, with an L5 source graph present) vs one provider in the thin fixture (public_header_ast only). Demonstrates that better corroboration comes from the combination of sources - the provider list ScanResult records grows with the evidence.

case152_enum_size_flag_flip.description: Identical source and exported symbols built once without -fshort-enums (v1) and once with it (v2). The captured L3 build options carry the flag; the build-evidence diff normalizes it to the canonical 'enum_size' option and reports enum_size_flag_changed. -fshort-enums shrinks every enum to the smallest integer that holds its range, so an enum member/parameter/return changes size and (as a struct member) shifts later fields. Symbol-only checks are blind; per ADR-028 D3 this is a risk signal, the artifact/type diff proves any concrete layout break.

case153_struct_packing_flip.description: Same source, same symbols, built once with -fpack-struct=8 (v1) and once with -fpack-struct=1 (v2). Struct packing's compiler default is target-dependent (GCC/Clang natural vs MSVC /Zp8), so a flip is reported only when both sides are explicit; the L3 build-evidence diff then routes the packing flag to struct_packing_mode_changed. Reduced packing removes padding, so every member offset and the type size can change with no source or symbol change; consumers compiled against the old packing read fields at stale offsets. A risk signal; the artifact/type diff proves the concrete offset break.

case154_lto_mode_flip.description: Identical source built once without LTO (v1) and once with -flto (v2). The build-evidence diff reports lto_mode_changed. LTO changes cross-TU inlining and can devirtualize or drop vtable/typeinfo emission, so the emitted symbol set and inlined public-inline bodies can differ from a non-LTO build. A risk signal to review; the artifact diff proves any concrete symbol/layout break.

case155_char_signedness_flip.description: Same source, same symbols, built once with -fsigned-char (v1) and once with -funsigned-char (v2). Because the default char signedness is target-dependent, both sides must be explicit; the build-evidence diff then reports char_signedness_changed. A plain-char parameter or member reinterprets the same bytes with the opposite sign, silently changing comparisons and value range in recompiled consumers. Only the captured build flag exposes it.

case156_public_macro_removed.description: The public header macro DEMO_MAX_ITEMS is present in the v1 source-replay surface and gone from v2. Macros never reach the binary, so no artifact layer sees the removal; only the L4 source surface does. Source that referenced the macro no longer compiles - a source/API break.

case157_inline_function_removed.description: The public header-only inline function demo::clamp is present in the v1 source-replay surface and removed in v2. Being inline it had no exported binary symbol, so the artifact diff (L0) is blind; only the L4 surface observes the lost declaration. Source that called the inline no longer compiles - a source/API break.

case158_public_typedef_removed.description: The public typedef demo::handle_t is present in v1 and removed in v2. A bare typedef emits no symbol of its own, so the artifact diff cannot see the removal; source replay surfaces it. Consumer source that named the alias no longer compiles - a source/API break.

case15_noexcept_change.note: noexcept change itself is COMPATIBLE; GLIBCXX version requirement is deployment risk

case160_public_api_internal_dep_added.description: In the derived L5 source graph the exported entry demo::parse newly calls the internal (non-public-header) helper detail::validate that it did not reach in v1. The public surface takes on an undeclared dependency, so a later change to the internal entity becomes a hidden risk to the API. The version-over-version analogue of the intra-version public-to-internal cross-check; a source-graph risk, never on its own an artifact-proven break.

case161_target_dependency_added.description: The L5 build graph gains a TARGET_DEPENDS_ON edge from libdemo to libcrypto between v1 and v2. The shipped artifact may now require an additional library at load time and takes on that dependency's ABI transitively, so a deployment that only shipped the old dependency set can fail to resolve at runtime. A packaging/deployment risk; the DT_NEEDED diff proves any concrete new load-time dependency.

case162_symbol_source_owner_changed.description: The exported symbol demo::init is declared by include/demo/legacy.h in v1 and by include/demo/init.h in v2, per the L5 source graph's SOURCE_DECLARES edge, while its name and signature are unchanged. The artifact diff is quiet, but the file owning the declaration behind a stable public symbol moved - review for include-path, inlining, or ODR effects. A source-graph risk, never on its own an artifact-proven break.

case163_python_kwarg_renamed.description: CPython extension whose .pyi renames a keyword-only argument (encoding -> codec) and drops its default; the compiled .so is byte-identical, so the C-ABI/abi3 check alone scores it compatible while the Python-level API breaks every keyword caller.

case164_preproc_conditional_field.description: A public struct field gains a #ifdef guard while both releases still ship with that macro defined, so the ABI is unchanged. A context-free header parse prunes the guarded field and raises a type_field_removed FALSE POSITIVE (the record size is artifact-accurate, so no size change fires); the binary depth is blind (identical builds) and correct. Only build context (the active -D define set, via --reconcile-build-context / ADR-039) proves the field is effectively present in both builds and clears the phantom. Ships committed guard-annotated snapshot fixtures (no compiler).

case165_polymorphic_nonvirtual_dtor.description: v2 adds a NEW polymorphic type (Exporter has a vtable via virtual write()) whose destructor is not virtual, plus a public factory returning Exporter* that callers delete. Deleting any future subclass through the base skips the derived destructor (UB). Existing binaries keep working — the finding is the ADR-027 anti-pattern POLYMORPHIC_TYPE_NON_VIRTUAL_DTOR, emitted as RISK only for newly introduced instances under --pattern-verdicts.
case165_polymorphic_nonvirtual_dtor.known_gap: macOS debug/header extraction currently misses the factory-based anti-pattern (the public factory's visibility is not resolved against the Mach-O export table the way the ELF path resolves it) and observes COMPATIBLE. Linux remains the blocking detector lane for polymorphic_type_non_virtual_dtor.

case166_ref_qualifier_added.description: str() gains an lvalue ref-qualifier (&) so that MessageBuilder().str() — a pointer into a dying temporary — no longer compiles. Sound API hardening, but the ref-qualifier is part of the Itanium mangling: _ZN14MessageBuilder3strEv becomes _ZNR14MessageBuilder3strEv, so every existing binary fails at load with an undefined symbol (FUNC_REF_QUAL_CHANGED).

case167_base_became_virtual.description: Stream's base Device became a VIRTUAL base (classic diamond preparation, iostream-style). The object model is rewritten: the Device subobject moves to the end, derived fields shift, sizeof grows, and Stream gains a VTT and vbase machinery. Old binaries read fields at dead offsets (BASE_CLASS_VIRTUAL_CHANGED).

case168_virtual_method_devirtualized.description: flush() was devirtualized: the exported symbol _ZN5Codec5flushEv survives with an identical signature (link and load succeed), but flush() left the vtable so every later slot shifts up one. Old binaries virtual-dispatch through the stale slot index and silently call reset() instead (FUNC_VIRTUAL_REMOVED); consumer overrides of flush() also silently stop being called.

case169_overload_added.description: units::to_celsius(double) — previously the unique declaration under its name — gains a float overload. Binary-compatible (the double symbol is untouched, old binaries keep working), but per the KDE BC policy this is a change to avoid: on the next recompile, float call sites silently re-route to the new overload (precision drift) and &units::to_celsius becomes ambiguous (OVERLOAD_ADDED).
case169_overload_added.known_gap: macOS (Mach-O export/visibility handling) and Windows (MSVC '?name@@...' mangling is not parsed by the Itanium overload grouper, whose documented fallback keeps unparseable symbols in distinct groups) currently miss OVERLOAD_ADDED and observe plain COMPATIBLE. Linux remains the blocking detector lane.

case170_env_runtime_floor_raised.description: Identical exported interface in both versions, but v2 was relinked on a newer distro: its libc.so.6 verneed set gains GLIBC_2.34 (newer than the old maximum GLIBC_2.28) because __libc_start_main rebinds to @GLIBC_2.34. The per-node symbol_version_required_added finding reports the fact; the runtime_floor_raised roll-up names the old->new deployment floor and the imported symbols that pulled it up. Environment drift (binutils/glibc skew), not an interface change: binary-compatible for existing consumers, but no longer loads on runtimes older than glibc 2.34. With a declared --env-matrix runtime floor the risk becomes decidable (>= 2.34 available everywhere -> COMPATIBLE; a declared 2.28 target -> BREAKING).

case171_static_tls_introduced.description: Identical source; v2 is built with -ftls-model=initial-exec instead of the default global-dynamic model. The linker sets DF_STATIC_TLS in the dynamic section because the library now participates in the static TLS surplus assigned at process startup, so it may no longer be reliably dlopen()able after the process has started. Read directly from DT_FLAGS with no DWARF, no headers, no build metadata (L0) -- the binary-only counterpart to case133_tls_model_flip's L3 build-flag view of the same transition.

case172_vtable_thunk_offset_changed.description: Derived multiply-inherits Base1 (primary) and Base2 (secondary), overriding Base2::f2() behind a non-virtual this-adjusting thunk. v2 adds a field to Base1, growing it and shifting Base2's subobject offset within Derived from 16 to 24 bytes -- Derived's method set and vtable slot count are completely unchanged, so only the thunk's baked-in offset (_ZThn16_ -> _ZThn24_) reveals the break. Detected from the _ZTh.../_ZTv.../_ZTc... symbol names on a stripped binary (L0). A consumer that static_cast<Base2*>()s a Derived* compiled against v1 computes the wrong pointer against v2's binary -- a deterministic crash.

case173_vtt_slot_count_changed.description: Diamond virtually inherits Base through both Left and Right in v1; v2 adds a third virtual-inheritance leg (Mixin), growing the VTT (_ZTT7Diamond, the construction-time scaffolding of sub-vtables used while a virtual-base object is being built) from 56 to 80 bytes. Co-occurs with vtable_slot_count_changed/rtti_inheritance_changed/vtable_thunk_offset_changed (already demonstrated elsewhere), but vtt_slot_count_changed is the only one that names the construction-time fact -- proven deterministically by comparing sizeof(Diamond) as compiled by an old consumer against the value the loaded library actually uses (16 vs 24 bytes), independent of whether any virtual method is ever called.

case174_secondary_vtable_group_changed.description: Derived : Base1, Base2 {} is byte-for-byte identical in v1 and v2 -- only Base2's own declaration changes, gaining a virtual method (non-polymorphic -> polymorphic). Because Base1 is Derived's primary base, Base2 (now polymorphic) needs a new secondary vtable group, restructuring Derived's dispatch surface even though Derived's own base list never moved -- a cross-type effect a per-type diff of Derived alone cannot see. Reconstructed from DWARF base/vtable metadata on both sides (L1); a stripped binary cannot reveal it, unlike cases 172/173.

case175_kabi_crc_changed.description: Two Module.symvers manifests (the Linux kernel's kABI record). ext4_iget keeps its symbol name, module, export type, and namespace but its CRC changes -- genksyms hashes the symbol's transitive type signature, so a CRC change means a type it depends on changed layout even though the symbol itself never disappeared. With CONFIG_MODVERSIONS on, the loader rejects an out-of-tree module whose embedded CRC disagrees ('disagrees about version of symbol'). Read directly from the two manifests with no kernel build and no compiler (L0).

case176_kabi_symbol_namespace_changed.description: Two Module.symvers manifests. drm_mode_object_add keeps its CRC, module, and GPL class but moves from a plain EXPORT_SYMBOL() to EXPORT_SYMBOL_NS(..., DRM), gaining an export namespace. A module that does not declare a matching MODULE_IMPORT_NS(DRM) fails to load even though the symbol's type signature never changed -- a distinct load-time gate from the CRC break in case175, with a different fix (add the import declaration, not rebuild against new types). Read directly from the two manifests (L0).

case177_long_double_abi_changed.description: compute()'s name and purpose are unchanged, but v2's library is rebuilt to use IEEE 754 quad precision (__float128, via libquadmath) instead of the platform's native 80-bit long double -- the same kind of representation swap real toolchains perform for long double on ppc64 (IBM double-double <-> IEEE binary128). Itanium mangles long double as 'e' and __float128 as 'g', so the exported symbol changes (_Z7computee -> _Z7computeg) even though nothing in the C++ declaration text needed to change spelling. abicheck re-pairs the removed/added symbols by their demangled signature into one long_double_abi_changed finding rather than an unrelated-looking remove+add (L0); an old consumer linked against _Z7computee fails to load with an undefined-symbol error.

case178_unnamed_type_in_public_abi.description: v2 adds pick_by_policy(), built from a namespace-scope lambda (descending) used as a default comparison policy -- a common header-only convenience pattern. Because the lambda has external linkage, its compiler-generated call operator is exported with default visibility, and its Itanium mangling embeds a per-translation-unit closure-type ordinal (Ul...E_) nobody chose: a trivial, unrelated source reorder can silently rename it on the next rebuild. Detected by parsing newly-introduced exported symbol names for Itanium unnamed-type tokens (L0); flagged only for symbols new this revision.

case179_cet_protection_weakened.description: Identical source; v1 is built with -fcf-protection=full and v2 with -fcf-protection=none. The .note.gnu.property x86 feature bits drop both IBT (indirect branch tracking) and SHSTK (shadow stack), silently regressing Intel CET hardening for dispatch() 's indirect function-pointer call even though the library behaves identically for legitimate input. Read directly from the ELF property note (L0); analysis-only under the default policy, escalatable to a hard gate under a security-focused profile.

source_smoke.proof: consumer runtime smoke: app compiled against v1 template layout fails when linked with v2 library

case180_symbol_binding_lost_unique.description: Identical source (a Meyers-singleton Registry<T>::instance() function-local static inside a class template); v2 is built with -fno-gnu-unique. The guard variable's ELF binding moves from STB_GNU_UNIQUE to an ordinary weak binding, losing the dynamic linker's process-wide uniqueness guarantee -- if the same template is independently instantiated by a second DSO loaded into the same process, each can end up with its own, diverging copy instead of sharing one. Read directly from the symbol table's binding field (L0).
case180_symbol_binding_lost_unique.known_gap: clang never emits STB_GNU_UNIQUE for vague-linkage template statics (a GCC ELF extension; clang always uses ordinary weak/guarded linkage), so no symbol_binding_lost_unique signal exists to detect under this toolchain

case181_xcheck_public_to_internal_depend.description: The public, header-declared json_parse() calls straight into validate_utf8(), a helper declared only in a private implementation file with no public declaration anywhere -- invisible from json_parse()'s own signature. The L5 source graph's DECL_CALLS_DECL edge (with a SOURCE_DECLARES edge giving the internal decl its file provenance) is the only way to see the dependency; if validate_utf8() changes behavior, json_parse()'s contract silently shifts with it. The intra-version counterpart to case150's exported_not_public/public_not_exported pair -- this one catches a mismatch inside a single public entry point rather than between the export table and the header set.

case20_enum_member_value_changed.reason: regular enum member value changes remain breaking; only named sentinel patterns are downgraded

source_smoke.proof: consumer runtime smoke: app compiled against old enum values fails with new library behavior

case32_param_defaults.description: Parameter default-value changes: connect() default 30->60 (changed), configure()'s verbose loses its default (removed -> source break), disconnect() gains a default (compatible). The mangled symbols are unchanged so binaries keep running; the break is source-only and detectable only in header mode (castxml Argument `default` attribute). Previously a documented gap (NO_CHANGE) until default-arg extraction was wired up.
case32_param_defaults.note: castxml on macOS/Homebrew does not emit the `default=` attribute on <Argument> elements, so the param_default_value_* detection only fires with the Linux castxml build. Scoped to linux to avoid a platform-dependent castxml gap (constant/finality extraction is unaffected and stays cross-platform).

case34_access_level.known_gap: macOS/Homebrew castxml currently drops the access-level transition for this fixture, so the macOS nightly observes NO_CHANGE. Linux remains the blocking detector lane.

case37_base_class.known_gap: macOS debug/header extraction currently misses this base-class change and observes NO_CHANGE in the nightly. Linux remains the blocking detector lane for type_base_changed.

source_smoke.proof: consumer runtime smoke: app compiled against v1 base-class layout fails with v2 library

case42_type_alignment_changed.description: Struct alignment changed via __attribute__((aligned(8))) to aligned(64) — stack allocation and array stride break (TYPE_ALIGNMENT_CHANGED)
case42_type_alignment_changed.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — without castxml, Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; type-level alignment changes are invisible

case43_base_class_member_added.description: Data member added to base class shifts derived class layout (libabigail: Added_Base_Class_Data_Member)
case43_base_class_member_added.known_gap: macOS debug/header extraction currently misses the base-class member addition and observes NO_CHANGE in the nightly. Windows observes COMPATIBLE for the same reason via validate_examples.py's CMake+MSVC build/dump/compare path (verified once the source_smoke shortcut that had been masking this platform's detector check stopped short-circuiting before it ran). Linux remains the blocking detector lane.

source_smoke.proof: consumer runtime smoke: app compiled against v1 derived/base layout fails with v2 library

case44_cyclic_type_member_added.description: Member added to a self-referential (cyclic) struct — sizeof changes (libabigail: Added_Non_Virtual_Member_Variable in cyclic type)

case45_multi_dim_array_change.description: Multi-dimensional array element type changed float->double — size doubles (libabigail: Subrange_Change)

case46_pointer_chain_type_change.description: Pointer chain ultimate type changes int->long through two indirection levels (libabigail: Function_Return_Type_Change indirect)

case47_inline_to_outlined.description: Inline method moved out-of-line: new exported symbol appears (FUNC_ADDED, libabigail: inline->outlined)

case48_leaf_struct_through_pointer.description: Leaf struct gains field; propagates through embedding in Container — layout breaks (libabigail: Leaf_Type_Change)

case49_executable_stack.description: Library linked with -Wl,-z,execstack has GNU_STACK RWE — disables NX protection (bad practice — security)

case50_soname_inconsistent.description: SONAME present but set to wrong major version — bakes wrong DT_NEEDED into consumers (bad practice — packaging/upgrade hazard)

case51_protected_visibility.description: Symbol visibility changed DEFAULT→PROTECTED — exported but no longer interposable (compatible, interposition policy concern)

case52_rpath_leak.description: Library has hardcoded build-directory path in DT_RUNPATH — non-portable deployment (bad practice — packaging)

case53_namespace_pollution.description: Library exports generic symbol names (init, process, cleanup) without prefix — collision risk (bad practice — API design)

case54_used_reserved_field.description: Reserved placeholder fields (__reserved1, __reserved2) renamed to meaningful names at same offsets — layout unchanged (USED_RESERVED_FIELD)
case54_used_reserved_field.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — without castxml, Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; struct field renames are invisible

case55_type_kind_changed.description: Type changed from struct to union — fields overlap instead of sequential layout (TYPE_KIND_CHANGED)
case55_type_kind_changed.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — without castxml, Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; struct-to-union type kind change is invisible

case56_struct_packing_changed.description: Struct packing changed via pragma pack(1) — field offsets and sizeof change (STRUCT_PACKING_CHANGED)
case56_struct_packing_changed.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — without castxml, Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; packing/layout changes are invisible

case57_enum_underlying_size_changed.description: Enum underlying type grows from int to long due to large sentinel value — sizeof changes (ENUM_UNDERLYING_SIZE_CHANGED)
case57_enum_underlying_size_changed.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — without castxml, Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; enum underlying size changes are invisible

case58_var_removed.description: Global variable lib_debug_level removed from export table (VAR_REMOVED)
case58_var_removed.known_gap: macOS: Mach-O export table does not distinguish functions from variables, so lib_debug_level can be detected as func_removed_elf_only instead of var_removed; both are breaking under strict binary policy.

case59_func_became_inline.description: Functions moved from outlined to static inline in header — symbols disappear from .dynsym (FUNC_REMOVED, inverse of case47)

case60_base_class_position_changed.description: Multiple inheritance base class order swapped — subobject offsets and vtable pointers change (BASE_CLASS_POSITION_CHANGED)
case60_base_class_position_changed.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — no headers provided; Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; base class layout changes are invisible

case61_var_added.description: New global variable lib_build_number added — purely additive (VAR_ADDED)

case62_type_field_added_compatible.description: Field added to opaque struct (callers use pointer only) — layout change invisible to consumers (TYPE_FIELD_ADDED compatible)

case63_bitfield_changed.description: Bitfield width changed in struct — all subsequent bitfields shift to different bit positions (FIELD_BITFIELD_CHANGED)

case64_calling_convention_changed.description: Function calling convention changed from default System V to ms_abi — parameters passed in wrong registers (CALLING_CONVENTION_CHANGED)
case64_calling_convention_changed.known_gap: GCC does not emit DW_AT_calling_convention for __attribute__((ms_abi)); detection requires Clang

case65_symbol_version_removed.description: ELF symbol version node CRYPTO_1.0 removed — old binaries linked against that version fail at load time (SYMBOL_VERSION_DEFINED_REMOVED)

case66_language_linkage_changed.description: extern "C" removed from C++ functions — symbol names change from unmangled to C++ mangled (FUNC_LANGUAGE_LINKAGE_CHANGED)

case67_tls_var_size_changed.description: Thread-local variable ErrorCtx grows from 68 to 72 bytes due to inserted field — struct field offsets shift, causing consumers to read wrong data (TLS_VAR_SIZE_CHANGED)

case68_virtual_method_added.description: Virtual method added to previously non-virtual class — vtable pointer inserted, sizeof grows, all field offsets shift (FUNC_VIRTUAL_ADDED)

case69_trivial_to_nontrivial.description: Trivially-copyable struct gains user-defined destructor — calling convention changes from register passing to hidden pointer (VALUE_ABI_TRAIT_CHANGED)
case69_trivial_to_nontrivial.known_gap: Windows MSVC x64: structs >8 bytes are always passed by pointer regardless of triviality, so the calling convention does not change

case70_flexible_array_member_changed.description: Flexible array member element type changed float → double — dynamic allocation size and element access offsets break while sizeof(Packet) stays the same (FLEXIBLE_ARRAY_MEMBER_CHANGED)

case71_inline_namespace_moved.description: Symbols moved from inline namespace v1 to v2 — mangled names change so old binaries fail to resolve symbols (INLINE_NAMESPACE_MOVED)

case72_covariant_return_changed.description: Intermediate class inserted into hierarchy — vtable layout shifts, object size changes, covariant return type changes from Circle* to Drawable* (TYPE_BASE_CHANGED)
case72_covariant_return_changed.known_gap: macOS debug/header extraction currently reports this hierarchy/covariant-return fixture as COMPATIBLE instead of BREAKING. Linux remains the blocking detector lane.

case73_typedef_underlying_changed.description: Typedef handle_t underlying type changed from int to void* — sizeof changes from 4 to 8 bytes, all functions using the typedef inherit the break (TYPEDEF_BASE_CHANGED)

case74_detail_base_class_changed.description: Internal-namespace leak: mylib::detail::descriptor_base gains a field; public mylib::knn_descriptor inherits from it, so sizeof/layout of the public class changes. Detected by existing type_size_changed/struct_field_added, escalated by internal_type_leaks_via_public_api.

case75_detail_embedded_by_value.description: Internal-namespace leak: mylib::detail::table_impl gains a field; public mylib::table embeds it by value, so sizeof(table) grows. Detected by existing struct_field_added on detail::table_impl, escalated by internal_type_leaks_via_public_api.

case76_detail_pimpl_vtable_changed.description: Internal-namespace leak: mylib::detail::algorithm_iface gains a virtual method inserted mid-vtable; public mylib::svm_algorithm inherits from it, so vtable slot indices shift for all consumers. Detected by existing type_vtable_changed / func_virtual_added, escalated by internal_type_leaks_via_public_api.
case76_detail_pimpl_vtable_changed.known_gap: macOS / Windows: vtable break is currently only detectable via symbol_size_changed on the ELF vtable symbol; Mach-O / PE export tables carry no symbol size, and castxml emits vtable_index=None for all virtuals so the structural diff collapses to a single slot — neither signal fires on these platforms

case77_detail_templated_base_changed.description: Internal-namespace leak through a templated detail:: base: mylib::detail::descriptor_base<Task> gains a field, growing every knn_descriptor<Task> instantiation. Verifies the leak reachability walker follows template-instantiation edges into detail::.

case78_task_arena_attach_tag.description: An `attach_mode_t` enum + matching constructor are replaced by an empty tag struct `task_arena::attach` + tag-dispatched constructor. The old enum, its members, and the old constructor's mangled symbol are all removed. Member of the oneTBB regression suite; mirrors the 2021 `task_arena::attach` tag introduction.

case79_missing_template_instantiation.description: Header advertises descriptor<float> and descriptor<double>; v2 library ships only the float instantiation. Header is unchanged so source compiles, link/load fails with undefined symbol. Emits INSTANTIATION_MISSING_FROM_BINARY.

case80_pimpl_shared_to_unique.description: Pimpl alias switched from shared_ptr<T> to unique_ptr<T>. The type of descriptor::impl_ changed; inline accessors re-mangle, destruction model changes (no atomic refcount), copy semantics flip from refcounted-share to move-only. Detected by existing type_field_type_changed escalated via internal_type_leaks_via_public_api.
case80_pimpl_shared_to_unique.known_gap: Windows: std::shared_ptr / std::unique_ptr layout names differ between MSVC STL and libstdc++/libc++, so the type-name-based detection signal is not portable as-shipped. Tracked for a follow-up that normalises smart-pointer template-arg names across stdlibs.

case81_serialization_tag_reassigned.description: DAAL-style serialization tag IDs swapped values between knn_model and linear_regression. Symbols, types, layout all unchanged — every conventional ABI check passes — but saved models silently deserialize as the wrong class. Emits SERIALIZATION_TAG_CHANGED.
case81_serialization_tag_reassigned.known_gap: Windows: the v1.h fixture includes ``<cstdint>`` for ``std::uint64_t``. On the GitHub Actions Windows runner, castxml's bundled Clang frontend can't parse mingw64 GCC 15.2.0's ``bits/c++config.h`` (it uses the C++23 ``bf16`` floating-point literal suffix: ``typedef __decltype(0.0bf16) __bfloat16_t;``). This is a toolchain incompatibility, not an issue with the detector or the example — castxml dump fails before the detector runs. Same root cause as case80 / case89.

case82_sycl_overload_set_removed.description: DPC++ build withdrawn between releases — every sycl::queue&-taking overload disappears across multiple algorithms. Reported as one grouped SYCL_OVERLOAD_SET_REMOVED finding instead of N independent func_removed.

case83_cpu_dispatch_isa_dropped.description: AVX-512 ISA tier dropped: every *_avx512_* symbol removed across all algorithms. Dispatcher-using callers unaffected; ISA-pinned callers break. Reported as one grouped CPU_DISPATCH_ISA_DROPPED finding (RISK_KINDS).
case83_cpu_dispatch_isa_dropped.known_gap: macOS Mach-O symbol evidence does not preserve the ELF-style ISA-dispatch grouping signal for this fixture; the nightly reports BREAKING instead of COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK. Linux remains the blocking detector lane.

case84_bundle_soname_skew.description: Multi-library bundle SONAME skew: libonedal_core and libonedal_dpc bump SONAME from .so.1 to .so.2 but libonedal_thread keeps .so.1 by accident. Per-library checks pass; cohort-level invariant fails. Emits BUNDLE_SONAME_SKEW.
case84_bundle_soname_skew.reason: Bundle case — no single-pair v1.cpp/v2.cpp layout; uses gen_bundle.sh to produce v1/ and v2/ directories. Validated end-to-end through `compare-release` in tests/test_bundle.py::TestCompareReleaseBundleE2E::test_compare_release_emits_soname_skew_for_case84; detect_bundle_soname_skew() also unit-tested in tests/test_cpp_pattern_detectors.py.

case85_internal_template_signature_chang.description: Internal helper template `lib::__detail::walk<T>` is dispatched through by a public inline algorithm `sum_range`. Between v1 and v2 the explicit instantiation set shifts (`walk<float>` removed, `walk<double>` added). Consumers compiled against v1 baked `walk<float>` into their symbol table; against v2 they fail to link. Function-template analogue of internal_type_leaks_via_public_api from PR #238.

case86_tag_struct_renamed.description: Empty tag struct method::brute_force renamed to method::search_brute. No layout signal (empty type), but every explicit descriptor<method::brute_force, ...> instantiation re-mangles. Emits TAG_TYPE_RENAMED correlating the empty type rename with the symbol-name shift.

case87_default_template_arg_changed.description: Default template argument changed (Distance = minkowski_distance → euclidean_distance). Consumer source compiles unchanged but substituted instantiation type differs, producing a different mangled symbol. Emits DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_ARG_CHANGED. Unlike case32 (function default param, NO_CHANGE), template defaults are part of the substituted type.

case88_cpo_kind_changed.description: Public name `lib::sort` is published in v1 as a free function and in v2 as a customisation point object (constexpr variable of an unspecified function-object class type). Call syntax keeps working but decltype, extern templates, and trait specialisations break.

case89_inline_accessor_renamed_pimpl_mem.description: Public inline accessor reaches into detail::descriptor_impl::class_count_ by name. v2 renames the field to n_classes_ and updates the inline body in lockstep, but consumers compiled against v1.h have the OLD field name baked into their inline accessor bodies. Emits INLINE_BODY_REFERENCES_RENAMED_MEMBER.
case89_inline_accessor_renamed_pimpl_mem.known_gap: Windows: the public class holds std::shared_ptr<detail::descriptor_impl> which renders differently in MSVC PDB/STL field-type strings, so the internal-leak reachability heuristic that ties the inline accessor to the renamed detail:: member does not fire as-shipped.

source_smoke.proof: consumer runtime smoke: app compiled against v1 inline accessor fails with v2 pimpl layout

case90_bundle_intra_dep_removed.description: Bundle (ADR-023): libcore removes core_mul; sibling libalgo still imports it (DT_NEEDED libcore.so.1 + U core_mul). Per-library compare reports libalgo NO_CHANGE; the bundle layer catches the dangling intra-bundle import.

case91_bundle_intra_signature_drift.description: Bundle (ADR-023): libcore changes core_add(int,int) -> core_add(long,long). extern C, mangled name unchanged. libalgo binary is byte-identical between versions but its call sites still use the int calling convention. Bundle layer flags libalgo as bundle_intra_dep_signature_changed.

case92_bundle_provider_changed.description: Bundle (ADR-023): shared_util moves from libcore to libutil between releases. The bundle's union of exported symbols is unchanged but DT_NEEDED contracts shift; downstream binaries that only linked -lcore now miss the symbol. Per-library worst-of reports BREAKING; the bundle layer adds COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK provider-change finding.

case93_bundle_manifest_drift.description: Bundle (ADR-023): manifest.yaml promises four train_* instantiations; the new bundle silently drops train_double_sparse. With --manifest, the bundle layer emits bundle_manifest_instantiation_removed. Without --manifest, the per-library func_removed already covers it but cannot distinguish 'promised' from 'incidental'.

case94_empty_tag_gained_state.description: An empty tag struct (mirrors tbb::auto_partitioner) gains a member; sizeof grows from 1 to 8. Any consumer that passed the tag by value into a header-inline algorithm interface now writes an undersized argument into a wider slot. Detected by the existing type_size_changed / type_field_added pair.

case95_allocator_nested_typedef_removed.description: An allocator-style class drops the historical STL nested-typedef set (value_type, pointer, reference, size_type, difference_type). Exported member symbols are unchanged so binaries still link, but consumer source using `typename Alloc::value_type` etc. fails to compile. Detected via `typedef_removed` (BREAKING per change_registry). Demonstrates the new `member_name` suppression selector — combined with `change_kind: typedef_removed`, it scopes the inevitable noise from removing a uniform set of nested aliases.
case95_allocator_nested_typedef_removed.known_gap: macOS/Homebrew castxml currently drops the nested typedef removal signal for this fixture, so the macOS nightly observes NO_CHANGE. Linux remains the blocking detector lane for typedef_removed.

case96_hidden_friend_removed.description: A non-member operator (`operator==`) declared as an in-class `friend` with an inline body (a 'hidden friend') is removed in v2. Consumers that wrote `a == b` resolved the call via ADL to the friend; with the declaration gone, ADL finds nothing and the source no longer compiles. The .so symbol set is unchanged because inline hidden friends typically have no external symbol. Detected via the new HIDDEN_FRIEND_REMOVED ChangeKind, derived from castxml's `befriending` attribute on the Class/Struct element.

source_smoke.proof: consumer hidden-friend smoke: old equality expression compiles, new header removes hidden friend

case97_api_depends_on_consumer_env.description: Header declares `lib::extended()` only when the consumer defines `USE_FEATURE`. The v1 build defines the macro and exports extended(); v2 builds without it. The matrix-aware API_DEPENDS_ON_CONSUMER_ENV finding is the channel that exposes this when both macro states are probed.

source_smoke.proof: consumer link smoke: old app links against v1 extended(), new headers still compile but v2 no longer exports it

case98_cxx_standard_floor_raised.description: The case has one ground-truth verdict: COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK. L3 build-context comparison detects std:CXX changing from gnu++17 to c++20 as abi_relevant_build_flag_changed. An L0-L2 context-free per-binary scan observes NO_CHANGE, which is a documented false negative from insufficient evidence, not an alternative expected verdict. L4/L5 source replay is not required.
case98_cxx_standard_floor_raised.known_gap: L0-L2 artifact-only scans cannot observe the C++ standard change because the binary surfaces are identical. Their NO_CHANGE verdict is a missed COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK finding caused by insufficient evidence, not a lane-specific expectation.

case99_experimental_graduated.description: A declaration lived only under lib::experimental:: in v1 and is published at the stable lib:: name in v2 while the experimental alias is retained. The dedicated EXPERIMENTAL_GRADUATED finding makes the migration event visible; the underlying signal is also a plain func_added for the stable name.

test_crosscheck_catalog: tests/test_g20_catalog.py — validates each G20 audit fixture against the case's canonical expected_kinds and provider assertions via run_crosschecks
